title: CropGraph Companion Planting Relationships
description:
The first edge layer of the CropGraph, directed relationships between crops (beneficial or antagonist) with a categorized mechanism, gardener-facing description, evidence-strength grade, and citation. Every `crop` and `companion` value is a slug from crop-calendar.json; the loader cross-references both at parse time and fails loud if a slug is missing.

Evidence grounding: companion planting literature ranges from peer-reviewed agricultural research to widely repeated traditional/folk knowledge. This dataset is curated against (a) USDA Cooperative Extension publications (Cornell, WSU, UC ANR, UF/IFAS, OSU, Penn State, Texas A&M), (b) USDA-ARS research on Three Sisters and allelopathic interactions, (c) Xerces Society pollinator research, (d) the canonical traditional sources (Riotte's `Carrots Love Tomatoes`, Cunningham's `Great Garden Companions`, the Rodale Encyclopedia), and (e) structural references like Plant Anywhere's relationship matrix and the Seeds for Generations matrix.

Strength grades are honest: `strong` is reserved for empirically supported relationships (Three Sisters nitrogen-fixation cycle, French marigold root-knot nematode suppression, allium / Rhizobium inhibition of legumes, nightshade-family disease sharing, peer-reviewed pollinator-attractor work). `moderate` covers widely-corroborated traditional pairings with at least some research support. `weak` (folk-only) entries are deliberately excluded from this fixture; community PRs can add them with proper citation in the future.

Relationships are directed because mechanisms are often asymmetric: corn provides structural support to climbing beans (corn → bean), and beans fix nitrogen for corn (bean → corn), two distinct edges, each with its own mechanism. Where the relationship is symmetric, one direction is stored and helpers expose a reverse-lookup fallback so callers don't need to know storage direction.

version.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes only; additive optional fields keep the version constant.

source.description: Provenance / citation block for the whole fixture. Per-entry `source` overrides this when present.

license.description: License under which the data is redistributed (e.g. CC-BY-4.0).

lastUpdated.description: ISO date of the last hand-curation pass.

entries.description: Directed relationship edges between crops. Order is not significant; helpers index by slug at load time.

crop.description: Subject crop slug. Must exist in crop-calendar.json.

companion.description: Related crop slug. Must exist in crop-calendar.json. May equal `crop` only in unusual cases (self-allelopathy), avoid where possible.

type.description: Whether the relationship helps or harms the subject crop.

mechanism.description: Categorized reason for the relationship. Curator judgment selects the dominant mechanism when several apply.

description.description: Gardener-facing explanation. Concrete and actionable, what the gardener should know or do, not just what the relationship is.

strength.description: Evidence grade. `strong` = empirical research support. `moderate` = widely-corroborated traditional + some research. `weak` = folk only. This fixture ships `strong` + `moderate` only; `weak` reserved for future community contributions.

source.description: Citation, extension service, research publication, or traditional reference. Multiple sources separated by semicolons.
